spec.name: Residential Lane
spec.description: West of the town square, the flagstones give way to an older residential lane where modest two-storey homes crowd together, their upper floors leaning slightly toward each other across the narrow street as if sharing a confidence. The buildings are timber-framed, their plaster washed in faded blues and yellows, and many of the ground-floor windows have small kitchen gardens or window boxes spilling herbs over the sills. The lane is quieter than the market streets, its sounds domestic: a shutter banging, the smell of something baking, children's voices from a courtyard behind one of the houses. Residents here know their neighbours in the particular way of people who have shared a wall for years — politely enough, and thoroughly. The cobbles are worn smooth in the walking lines, and the street has no formal name; most maps just mark it as the residential quarter.
